summary: The test `test-debugger-exec` timed out while waiting for the debugger to break on start. The debugger attached successfully but never emitted the expected break-on-start message within the 15-second timeout window.
root_cause: The debugger test `test/parallel/test-debugger-exec.js` timed out (15000ms) waiting for the pattern `/break (?:on start )?in/i` to appear in the debugger output. The debugger connected and attached but did not pause at the initial breakpoint as expected.

evidence.quote: Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i
evidence.reason: Explicit timeout error showing the test waited 15 seconds for the debugger break-on-start signal but never received it.
Stack trace shows the failure occurred in the `waitForInitialBreak` helper, confirming the debugger never broke on start.
The output shows the debugger attached and presented the prompt, but no break-on-start message was emitted, explaining the timeout.
Confirms exactly one test failed in the test run.
The flaky-test retry mechanism ran the test 10 times and it failed 1 time, indicating this is a timing/flaky issue.

suggested_fix: This appears to be a flaky/timing issue where the debugger attaches but does not emit the initial break event in time on macOS CI. Consider increasing the TIMEOUT value in `test/common/debugger.js` or investigating why the debugger does not pause at the initial breakpoint on this platform/version.
